content: imo the reason they probably see it as a problem is most people spend for a specific character they like. And with 600 pulls you likely get OE and their WoD from just that. Which for a lot of people is enough and sure some will spend to get +12 but a lot of newer people that just like that one character probably won’t. From the losses they can make a decent team and not feel any need to spend for a while. Sure they’ll likely spend on another character in the long run but that’s in my opinion why they see it as a problem. Since it will result in newer players spending a lot less on the game than they would have originally to get OE for that character they enjoy.

content:
This might a bit late to reach you <@1487626955791663208>  light sama but 

I largely consider the state of pandia and alva to be the recession markers of balance.

It's kinda like how with gintama, the second madao gets a job, the series is considered coming close to ending. 

The day that these two characters perform in a way that is similar to the levels of other limited characters, that means the average power of the cast is close to each other.

That is because alva's kit is inherent close to the base gameplay of morimens, while pandia reflects the most basic level of extra mechanics. (Strength stacking/passive damage) 

The futher these two are from the rest of the cast, the clearer you are able to see the game's general power gap. 

I still have faith in you and I am looking forward to these devlogs that will detail plans going forward. 

This is the only way for us to understand what you are trying to do and you can have the time to either alleviate our concerns or take our feedback before you invest development resources into a system.


While you say that it is not your game but ours.

It isn't. It is both. 

Your vision backed by our passion. That is our relationship as gamers to developers. 

We do not wish to hijack or take the helm of your visions. But like every form of work, us players are many so we have many views that we can give to you so that you can choose a path forward.

I would rather feel like we are together in the trenches trying to keep morimens going, than it turning into a tug of war. 

Please keep these sentiments in mind for us. It is a matter of we. Not you or I.
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
在校貓走之前我很平靜的說：

我覺得你在拿ai敷衍我，不過沒關係，至少你回了，我可以放心了。那麼，未來能不能讓你們的計劃表公開透明？這個版本會做什麼，那個小更新打算加什麼，提前告訴我們再實裝也不用浪費產能改來改去🧐

週邊不復刻沒關係，能不能別用官圖出谷？看膩官圖了，給點週邊限定的圖然後給一個兌換宿舍背景的兌換碼就完美了

Before the school cat left, I calmly said:

I feel like you're just using AI to brush me off, but it's okay, at least you replied, so I can relax. So, could you please make your future plans more transparent? What will this version do? What are you planning to add in that small update? Let us know in advance so we don't waste production capacity on endless revisions. 🧐

It's fine if the merchandise isn't re-released, but could you please stop using official images for the release? I'm tired of official images. Just give me some images of limited-edition merchandise and a redemption code for a dormitory background, and that would be perfect.(love from Google translate💀)

content:
Am tired, just Google translate it yourself if you want to understand, I'll try not to use some words that hard to translate. I am so fucking polite this time.

其實怎麼說呢，我對校貓的感情很複雜，一方面不想罵他是基於和他互動時的好感和一些過往的美好回憶，但另一方面又覺得這傢伙沒在進步甚至退步，恨鐵不成鋼的想罵他……他過往真的做過一些贏了我尊重和信任的事，也真的對他很有好感，相信着這個經常搞砸卻依然能帶着🧠團隊和玩家走得遠的傢伙能做出一番成就。

但是他這次真的沒辦法讓我保持對他的尊重和敬愛，他說他做了欠缺考慮的事情，但單單在我眼裡就已經代表着他光說會聽卻沒有真正聽取過社群的意見，社區裡滿是真正用錢支持他們、真正切身體驗遊戲、真正為遊戲做各種(包括同人創作和攻略)貢獻、真正熱愛遊戲的人。他的做法會讓人覺得他只想要取悅貨幣匯率較高的地區的人，既然是出來認錯、擋炮火的，道歉和回覆就該認真且有誠意，而不是用ai生成一個中文回覆，他明明會中文的。可以用ai潤色，但你不能讓人發現你用了ai，不然只會引起更多反對的聲浪，因為在這個世代用ai本身就代表敷衍。

但冷靜地提意見被無視，帶着一些悲傷或者憤怒地想要質詢的情緒的發言又會被製作人視為「不冷靜的發言」……是的，他遭受了很多謾罵，但一開始的謾罵裡也依舊夾雜着建議、或者他們認為製作人哪裡做得不好的觀點。

我是很認真的因為light的一系列操作而失去信任，我很失望。
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
怕太長貓不會看，我盡量縮短一點 

1.
既然要推最終法則了，角色體驗關卡設個選擇啟靈數，體驗開個可配備隊伍和為角色設置啟靈數，讓我們有一個直觀的方法知道你賣的+12值不值。

2.
體驗那邊或者喚醒體界面，可不可以有遊戲內的配置+配隊推薦和點讚推薦的功能？或者給角色點讚？不設角色留言了，感覺會增加管理成本。給角色點讚至少可以讓你們對角色廚數量有概念，方便出週邊。

3.
最終法則方面，比起數值加強，也許我們會更在意別的東西。都是角色廚/強度廚才會抽滿吧，那給個+12限定角色頭像/頭像框、個人界面背景裝飾、或者額外的繁衍狂熱劇情/角色故事，就這些可能五選二之類的推出來都能接受，只有通訊錄不夠吸引角色廚。

4.
這麼大的改動，不僅是要提前說，甚至應該在實裝之前先拿出來說。讓我們討論，這樣你又可以發現更多應改未改或者應有未有的東西。讓未來的計劃公開透明，數據留痕，下次(如果有)企劃或者角色設計抄襲事件，不需要你出手，自然就會有玩家維護你和我們的遊戲。

5.
想要典藏館/喚醒體界面放戰鬥小人讓我們可以放大看細節，也想要放大看看一些怪物設計，想要可以查看怪物和他們進攻動畫的功能，因為一些怪物真的很酷。

6.
我愛你和我們的遊戲，校貓，但是你真的讓人愛得很累，下次再有這樣的事我真的不會罵，我會用實質的行動證明我已經完全對你失去信任。即使你用ai敷衍我也沒關係，我最後再信你一次。但真的是最後一次，不要逼我離開我愛的角色和遊戲。
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
# 忘卻前夜老玩家已成為遊戲發展的阻礙

就應該趕走這些玩了一、二年還反過來黑忘卻的老玩家。
今年真的離大譜，往年是水軍帶節奏，今年是那些老登們帶節奏，真的噁心煩人，要是對手水軍派來擾亂社區也就罷了，可一個個的玩了大幾百天還被人家帶偏，只能說這些老登們是最難伺候的。
最開始帶產能節奏，然後是皮膚的節奏，現在又在瘋狂地帶角色強度甚至最終法則的節奏，自家遊戲自己不護著，一個個的什麼玩意呀，一個個的自私自利，一點都不顧及零氪玩家和萌新的感受。如果能這些老登趕出忘卻前夜，社區環境會好的多。

content:
“what I put in matches what I get back” 
What you've said before made me believe that you're genuinely trying to balance this ideal with still making whaling appealing so that you can profit, which I get. So OE is lower investment but you can keep going after that up to a high dupe rate. Now with adding the axioms the progression doesn't make any sense. You have a huge gap between OE and Axiom and Axiom is 99% for whales which gives them a huge power spike after a huge investment. It sounds good for them but not for any other part of progression. It needs to be more even, your original intent of having amount of allowed dupes so high doesn't fit in this new axion system. The total dupes progression needs to be squashed. And also it's NOT a good idea to stick with OP axioms just because you already announced them. You need to balanced them down before release.
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
no way they are going to balance them “down” before release, just look at the first paragraph of the apology letter, this AA system is a means to make money. 

why would they nerf a AA if it’s good and will encourage more players to pull for it ?

also, they are only apologising for not communicating the AA patch sooner, not for using AA as a means to make more $$. 

if community feedback ever mattered to the devs, they would have started a survey weeks, heck, months ago when they were planning this AA system 

but it didn’t happen. and that says a lot. they will do what they want, and if there is backlash they’ll just placate players with apologies and some free silver / pulls, while not changing or reverting any absurd things they added

thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
"makes more pay to win, sets a band precedent, think about the future" - are all hypotheticals.

B.I.A.V. as far as we know is not restrained by any investor demands. The only thing they have is their own motivation and the playerbases wishes.

If they like every other gacha were held down by investor and moneymaking expectations. None of these or any of the other choices in the past would have been reverted.

Some OE's already make the game boring to use. P2W is not determined by a unit being overpowered, but main content being unclearable without investment.

I feel like it's genuenly, brain dead to make hypotheticals about a company that owes anyone, outside the players nothing. In their attempt to grow.

If someone wants to AA an op unit and to clear the game easily they should be able to. It's just one more of the countless options in a game with many countless options already.

Granted again people may complain about no content for their op unit. But it was their choice to get to that point. (Plus the Devs can just add the ability to disable enlightens).

Obviously another discussion that comes in mind is P2W Vs f2p content.

Then the Devs decided to add the limited cores. But without telling anywhere outside the patch notes us, added a way to craft selectors. Again, encouraging interaction with the endgame game mode, allowing you to neglect some part of the grind.

I'm genuenly getting tired of all the bitching from the playerbase. Since BIAV owes nothing to no-one. Any change that would ACTUALLY IMPACT HOW YOU PLAY THE GAME FOR THE WORSE. Could be rolled back.

Everyone knows that they would have still played the game with these changes. The greed rope still has a lot of give. It's just instant hate and crying over preconceived notions in the gacha genre again. (1/2)

thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
So are we going to wait till the game almost EOS's again. Overworked Devs get sick and more people quick cause of constant overwork. Or will anyone actually bother to remember why they actually like this game and it's design, and think about if these changes even impact their experiences outside of made up hypotheticals.

I know better then most when it comes to this, cause I had crashed out during the 2nd aniv story balancing. And inevitably, I can comfortably say that at the time I was wrong about the direction of the game...

I still feel the fights in ch9 lost all impact or any clever account wide teambuilding, and none of the arc2 bosses stand up to the gameplay design experience that clearing ch9 was, but for the rest of it. seeing friends start the game, I'm satisfied.

And that's the sort of outlook I have in general about these updates.

They thought about it for a reason. And they thought about it long. An initial freak out based on a bunch of made up info, with no experience or knowledge behind the scenes, means nothing in comparison.

And the Devs should put their foot down at some point. If the situation is bad enough. This game is already way too generous anyways, and if that's unsurvivable, or it makes it impossible to get even close to the quality of arc1 content... I'd rather have the game equal the greed of the rest of the industry.

[Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
That's very brave and nice of you, but most gacha games are able to do this because of the sheer weigth of their massive playerbases. 
Morimens has also not been growing which proves people who stick their head in aren't sticking around.

And now new updates are alienating a noticable portion of existing players on top of just trying to monetize them harder through gameplay mechanics, which historically breeds resentment.

You can tout the idea "be as greedy as other gachas", but that only works if players stick around to spend as much as is asked of them.

The existing players have to complain because the alternative is quitting a game they love and letting it die, and the devs have to change their overall strategy based on player wants because the game is not growing and their main source of advertisement is word of mouth.

thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
I don't understand how players wanting better communication about huge changes like AA is considered bitching and moaning. 

Especially when Light said before that those AAs would not affect a character's strength in a significant way. And yet, the most broken units get equally broken AAs while others are left to dust. 

We've already seen the hp inflation in dtide with the release of GMurphy. Or the release of more broken units after Tawil and Ghelot, when the devs said they wouldn't do again. 

The bitching and moaning is because people still care about the game. I would be more afraid of its future if they released such changes and everyone would just accept them.

content:
In my opinion, the best way for them to get money would be merch. Like, that's what almost everyone has been asking for. Even some friends of mine, who never have spended on the game, wanted to buy some merch, but it was impossible.

There's several games that live off merch sales, Azur Lane being one example. They don't even need to make gameplay changes, they simply release merch, and people that like the games and it's characters buy it.

I also wanted to try and buy, but it's impossible if you're from Europe. So it's not that people don't want to spend money, but that they can't.

content:
Let's assume for a second they deserve to be as greedy as the industry standard.
Were just out of a full rerun patch. Full rerun patches are, in industry standard, dead patches that bring a tenth of the revenue a unit patch would bring.

Concerning merchandise, there are ways to buy them through proxy services. I won't name them, no point advertising.
But I can't even find the morimens shop through the proxy services.
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
There's any number of things, but generalised you 1. ensure existing players are satisfied.

Off the top of my head, buff/fix existing characters to be usable without hiding it behind a monetisation scheme.

2. Ensure the new-player experience leaves a good impression. For example 10 different menus of notifications you don't understand or care about and constant pop ups asking you to spend leaves a bad first impression. 

The devs would probably have means to more concretely check where players end up quitting and infer what did it, so the actual changes would depend.

What we have instead is a messy first-impression of menus and game modes with updates focused on 'almost' giving existing players things they have asked for, for ages and a monkeys paw style, but. Like this most recent update technically buffing characters, _but_ you need max duplicates.

This game is also not far from a capital G, gacha despite the generosity of redeems, but it would be healthy to take ideas and inspiration from other games outside the gacha sphere and other games that have had consistent growth inside the gacha sphere.
